evals.prompt: Use an agent chatroom to pressure-test whether we should split a monolith into services. I want three distinct roles, at most two rounds, and a final recommendation.
evals.expected_behavior: Uses a multi-agent debate workflow with distinct roles, a bounded round structure, and a synthesis that calls out agreement, disagreement, and the recommended action.
assertions: Uses a debate or chatroom workflow rather than independent polling
Mentions a bounded round structure or early convergence
Produces a final synthesis or recommendation

evals.prompt: Have an architect, a security reviewer, and a pragmatist debate whether we should ship the new billing worker to all users immediately or release it behind a feature flag first, then tell me the strongest disagreement that remains.
evals.expected_behavior: Preserves the requested roles, treats the task as a multi-perspective debate, and reports the most important remaining disagreement alongside a synthesis.
assertions: Frames the work as multi-round or explicitly interactive discussion
Surfaces the strongest disagreement separately from the recommendation
Avoids presenting the task as a simple solo opinion
